rag-security-readiness-review/02_evidence/phase_5/frontend_architecture.md:53:The frontend is a Next.js application organized by routes and feature areas. It has separate pages for login, the main chat app, admin functions, and the craft/build experience. Shared frontend services and UI components live in library and section directories. API and MCP requests have dedicated route handlers.

rag-security-readiness-review/02_evidence/phase_5/authorization_flow_map.md:1:# PHASE 5 Authorization Flow Map
rag-security-readiness-review/02_evidence/phase_5/authorization_flow_map.md:4:This map identifies role, permission, group, token-scope, tenant, admin-gate, and frontend UI-gating evidence in repository files.
rag-security-readiness-review/02_evidence/phase_5/authorization_flow_map.md:7:- In scope: `backend/onyx/auth/permissions.py`, `backend/onyx/auth/users.py`, `backend/onyx/server/auth_check.py`, `backend/onyx/db/enums.py`, `backend/onyx/db/models.py`, authorization-relevant route files under `backend/onyx/server/`, Enterprise DB authorization helpers under `backend/ee/onyx/db/`, shared tenant files under `shared_configs/`, and frontend admin/permission routing files under `web/src/`.
rag-security-readiness-review/02_evidence/phase_5/authorization_flow_map.md:8:- Out of scope: live group membership, live tenant assignments, external IdP authorization rules, and manual admin procedures not represented in repository files.
rag-security-readiness-review/02_evidence/phase_5/authorization_flow_map.md:11:- Permission dependency factory and effective-permission logic: `backend/onyx/auth/permissions.py`.
rag-security-readiness-review/02_evidence/phase_5/authorization_flow_map.md:12:- Current-user and token-scope handling: `backend/onyx/auth/users.py`.
rag-security-readiness-review/02_evidence/phase_5/authorization_flow_map.md:13:- Route auth verification helper: `backend/onyx/server/auth_check.py`.
rag-security-readiness-review/02_evidence/phase_5/authorization_flow_map.md:14:- Permission enum: `backend/onyx/db/enums.py`.
rag-security-readiness-review/02_evidence/phase_5/authorization_flow_map.md:15:- User, group, permission, connector, document-set, credential, persona, and MCP relationship models: `backend/onyx/db/models.py`.
rag-security-readiness-review/02_evidence/phase_5/authorization_flow_map.md:16:- Admin/security route examples: `backend/onyx/server/security/api.py`, `backend/onyx/server/manage/users.py`, `backend/onyx/server/features/persona/api.py`, `backend/onyx/server/features/tool/api.py`, `backend/onyx/server/features/mcp/api.py`.
rag-security-readiness-review/02_evidence/phase_5/authorization_flow_map.md:17:- Tenant context/config: `backend/shared_configs/contextvars.py`, `backend/shared_configs/configs.py`, `backend/onyx/utils/tenant.py`.
rag-security-readiness-review/02_evidence/phase_5/authorization_flow_map.md:18:- Frontend admin route metadata and permission-aware UI code: `web/src/lib/admin-routes.ts`, `web/src/sections/modals/languageModels/shared.tsx`.
rag-security-readiness-review/02_evidence/phase_5/authorization_flow_map.md:21:1. `backend/onyx/db/enums.py` defines permission enum values.
rag-security-readiness-review/02_evidence/phase_5/authorization_flow_map.md:22:2. `backend/onyx/db/models.py` defines users, user groups, permission grants, and relationship tables that connect users/groups to documents, document sets, credentials, skills, personas, tools/MCP, and LLM providers.
rag-security-readiness-review/02_evidence/phase_5/authorization_flow_map.md:23:3. `backend/onyx/auth/permissions.py` expands implied permissions and exposes `require_permission()` as a FastAPI dependency factory.
rag-security-readiness-review/02_evidence/phase_5/authorization_flow_map.md:24:4. Route files apply authorization by using user dependencies or `require_permission()` dependencies.
rag-security-readiness-review/02_evidence/phase_5/authorization_flow_map.md:25:5. `backend/onyx/server/auth_check.py` inspects routes for auth dependencies, which is evidence of route-level auth coverage checking.
rag-security-readiness-review/02_evidence/phase_5/authorization_flow_map.md:26:6. Tenant context is represented by shared context/config files and backend tenant utility modules.
rag-security-readiness-review/02_evidence/phase_5/authorization_flow_map.md:27:7. Frontend route/menu gating evidence is represented by admin route metadata and UI code that references roles or agents/groups.
rag-security-readiness-review/02_evidence/phase_5/authorization_flow_map.md:30:- `backend/onyx/auth/permissions.py` — permission implication map, effective permission calculation, `require_permission()` dependency.
rag-security-readiness-review/02_evidence/phase_5/authorization_flow_map.md:31:- `backend/onyx/auth/users.py` — current-user dependencies and token-scope logic.
rag-security-readiness-review/02_evidence/phase_5/authorization_flow_map.md:32:- `backend/onyx/server/auth_check.py` — route dependency inspection for auth coverage.
rag-security-readiness-review/02_evidence/phase_5/authorization_flow_map.md:33:- `backend/onyx/db/enums.py` — `Permission` enum and other auth-related enums.
rag-security-readiness-review/02_evidence/phase_5/authorization_flow_map.md:34:- `backend/onyx/db/models.py` — `User`, `UserGroup`, `PermissionGrant`, relationship models, and scoped domain entities.
rag-security-readiness-review/02_evidence/phase_5/authorization_flow_map.md:35:- `backend/onyx/server/security/api.py` — route file using full-admin permission dependency.
rag-security-readiness-review/02_evidence/phase_5/authorization_flow_map.md:36:- `backend/onyx/server/manage/users.py` — user/admin management routes.
rag-security-readiness-review/02_evidence/phase_5/authorization_flow_map.md:37:- `backend/onyx/server/features/persona/api.py`, `backend/onyx/server/features/tool/api.py`, `backend/onyx/server/features/mcp/api.py` — feature routes where permission checks can be reviewed.
rag-security-readiness-review/02_evidence/phase_5/authorization_flow_map.md:38:- `backend/shared_configs/contextvars.py`, `backend/shared_configs/configs.py`, `backend/onyx/utils/tenant.py` — tenant context/config evidence.
rag-security-readiness-review/02_evidence/phase_5/authorization_flow_map.md:39:- `web/src/lib/admin-routes.ts`, `web/src/sections/modals/languageModels/shared.tsx` — frontend admin/role-aware UI evidence.
rag-security-readiness-review/02_evidence/phase_5/authorization_flow_map.md:42:- Internal dependencies: authorization code depends on DB enum/model definitions, current-user dependencies, route modules, and tenant context helpers.
rag-security-readiness-review/02_evidence/phase_5/authorization_flow_map.md:43:- External dependencies supported by files: none required to state authorization architecture; live IdP/group sync integrations require deeper review of connector/auth integration files.
rag-security-readiness-review/02_evidence/phase_5/authorization_flow_map.md:47:- A complete per-route authorization matrix requires deeper handler-by-handler review.
rag-security-readiness-review/02_evidence/phase_5/authorization_flow_map.md:50:Authorization is modeled through users, roles, permission enums, user groups, relationship tables, token scopes, and FastAPI dependencies. Backend route files can require specific permissions. Frontend admin navigation and role-aware screens provide UI-level gating, but backend permission checks are the authoritative evidence for authorization behavior.
rag-security-readiness-review/02_evidence/phase_5/authorization_flow_map.md:55:| Permissions are modeled as enum values and expanded through backend logic. | `backend/onyx/db/enums.py`, `backend/onyx/auth/permissions.py` | Supported | Permission enum and implication map exist. |
rag-security-readiness-review/02_evidence/phase_5/authorization_flow_map.md:56:| Route handlers can require permissions through a FastAPI dependency. | `backend/onyx/auth/permissions.py`, `backend/onyx/server/security/api.py` | Supported | `require_permission()` and full-admin route dependency evidence exist. |
rag-security-readiness-review/02_evidence/phase_5/authorization_flow_map.md:57:| Users, groups, permission grants, and domain relationships are persisted models. | `backend/onyx/db/models.py` | Supported | Model classes include users, user groups, permission grants, and join tables. |
rag-security-readiness-review/02_evidence/phase_5/authorization_flow_map.md:58:| Token scopes participate in permission decisions. | `backend/onyx/auth/users.py`, `backend/onyx/auth/permissions.py` | Supported | Auth and permission files reference token scope handling. |
rag-security-readiness-review/02_evidence/phase_5/authorization_flow_map.md:59:| Tenant context has source-level support. | `backend/shared_configs/contextvars.py`, `backend/shared_configs/configs.py`, `backend/onyx/utils/tenant.py` | Supported | Tenant context/config utility files exist. |
rag-security-readiness-review/02_evidence/phase_5/authorization_flow_map.md:60:| Frontend admin route/UI gating is represented in source files. | `web/src/lib/admin-routes.ts`, `web/src/sections/modals/languageModels/shared.tsx` | Supported | Admin metadata and role/agent/group-aware UI files exist. |
rag-security-readiness-review/02_evidence/phase_5/authorization_flow_map.md:64:- Live user/group permissions and a complete per-route authorization matrix are unsupported without runtime data and a deeper route audit.

rag-security-readiness-review/02_evidence/phase_5/agent_tool_mcp_map.md:22:1. Agent/persona APIs are defined under `backend/onyx/server/features/persona/` and backed by persona DB helper/model files.

rag-security-readiness-review/02_evidence/phase_5/agent_tool_mcp_map.md:24:3. MCP APIs are defined under `backend/onyx/server/features/mcp/`, with MCP server support under `backend/onyx/mcp_server/` and persistent MCP models/helpers under `backend/onyx/db/models.py` and `backend/onyx/db/mcp.py`.

rag-security-readiness-review/02_evidence/phase_5/agent_tool_mcp_map.md:26:5. `web/src/app/mcp/[[...path]]/route.ts` provides a frontend MCP route handler separate from the general API route.

rag-security-readiness-review/02_evidence/phase_5/agent_tool_mcp_map.md:34:- `backend/onyx/tools/` — tool implementation package.
rag-security-readiness-review/02_evidence/phase_5/agent_tool_mcp_map.md:35:- `backend/onyx/db/models.py`, `backend/onyx/db/mcp.py`, `backend/onyx/db/tools.py`, `backend/onyx/db/persona.py` — persistence helpers and entity definitions for these domains.

rag-security-readiness-review/02_evidence/phase_5/agent_tool_mcp_map.md:37:- `web/src/app/admin/actions/page.tsx`, `web/src/app/admin/agents/page.tsx`, `web/src/sections/actions/` — frontend UI surfaces.
rag-security-readiness-review/02_evidence/phase_5/agent_tool_mcp_map.md:40:- Internal dependencies: persona/agent routes depend on DB persona helpers, tool routes depend on DB tool helpers and tool implementations, MCP routes depend on DB MCP helpers and MCP server support files.
rag-security-readiness-review/02_evidence/phase_5/agent_tool_mcp_map.md:41:- External dependencies supported by files: MCP protocol/server integration is represented by the MCP server package and MCP route files; external OpenAPI/custom tool behavior is represented by frontend `openApiService` and backend tool feature files.
rag-security-readiness-review/02_evidence/phase_5/agent_tool_mcp_map.md:44:- Live MCP server connections, enabled tools, per-user credentials, and execution results are runtime data and are not known from repository files.
rag-security-readiness-review/02_evidence/phase_5/agent_tool_mcp_map.md:45:- Whether a given agent can use a given tool in production requires live configuration and permission review.
rag-security-readiness-review/02_evidence/phase_5/agent_tool_mcp_map.md:48:Agents/personas, tools/actions, and MCP are implemented as separate but connected areas. The backend has APIs and database helpers for each, and the frontend has admin pages and service helpers that surface them. MCP also has a dedicated frontend route handler and backend MCP server package.
rag-security-readiness-review/02_evidence/phase_5/agent_tool_mcp_map.md:53:| Agent/persona APIs are a backend feature area. | `backend/onyx/server/features/persona/api.py`, `backend/onyx/server/features/persona/models.py` | Supported | Persona feature API/model files exist. |
rag-security-readiness-review/02_evidence/phase_5/agent_tool_mcp_map.md:54:| Tool/action APIs are a backend feature area. | `backend/onyx/server/features/tool/api.py`, `backend/onyx/server/features/tool/models.py`, `backend/onyx/tools/` | Supported | Tool route/model package and implementations exist. |
rag-security-readiness-review/02_evidence/phase_5/agent_tool_mcp_map.md:55:| MCP has backend feature APIs and MCP server support files. | `backend/onyx/server/features/mcp/api.py`, `backend/onyx/mcp_server/api.py`, `backend/onyx/mcp_server_main.py` | Supported | MCP feature and server files exist. |
rag-security-readiness-review/02_evidence/phase_5/agent_tool_mcp_map.md:56:| Agent/tool configuration files exist. | `backend/onyx/configs/agent_configs.py`, `backend/onyx/configs/tool_configs.py` | Supported | Dedicated config files exist. |
rag-security-readiness-review/02_evidence/phase_5/agent_tool_mcp_map.md:57:| Frontend exposes admin/service plumbing for agents, actions, tools, and MCP. | `web/src/app/admin/actions/page.tsx`, `web/src/app/admin/agents/page.tsx`, `web/src/lib/tools/mcpService.ts`, `web/src/lib/tools/openApiService.ts`, `web/src/sections/actions/` | Supported | UI and service files exist. |
rag-security-readiness-review/02_evidence/phase_5/agent_tool_mcp_map.md:58:| Live MCP/tool enablement and execution results are known. | No repository evidence identified. | Unsupported | Runtime configuration and execution logs are not present. |
